title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored

input_row.title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by multi-factor authentication (MFA).
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring for single-factor console logins will increase visibility into accounts that are not protected by MFA. These type of accounts are more susceptible to compromise and unauthorized access.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: • aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: • aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: • aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }" Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }" 6. Note the <no-mfa-console-signin-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <no-mfa-console-signin-metric> captured in step 4. 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName== <no-mfa-console-signin-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS Management Console sign-ins without MFA and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1. 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }' Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-alarm>` --metric-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist for console sign-ins without MFA.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored Filter pattern set to { ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success"}: • reduces false alarms raised when a user logs in via SSO

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_metric_filter_no_mfa_console_signin

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
This automated program will perform the following checks:

1. **Verify Active Multi-Region CloudTrail Trail:**
   - Use the AWS CLI command `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to list all trails and check for:
     - Multi-region trails (`IsMultiRegionTrail` is true).
     - Active status (`IsLogging` is true when calling `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`).
     - Management events (`IncludeManagementEvents` is true and `ReadWriteType` is set to All using `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`).

2. **Retrieve Log Group Name:**
   - Extract the `<trail-log-group-name>` from `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn` in the multi-region trail details.

3. **Check for Metric Filters:**
   - Execute `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` to retrieve existing metric filters and ensure it includes:
     - The specified `filterPattern` for console logins without MFA.
 
   - For example, the expected filter pattern may resemble:
     ```
     { ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }
     ```

4. **Validate CloudWatch Alarms:**
   - Use the command `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName== <no-mfa-console-signin-metric>]'` to check if there are any associated alarms for the metric filter found in the previous step.

5. **Verify SNS Subscription:**
   - For the identified SNS topic from the alarm, execute `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` to ensure there is at least one active subscriber with a valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- The audit will pass if:
  - At least one multi-region CloudTrail trail is active and logs are being captured.
  - The trail captures all management events.
  - At least one metric filter exists to monitor for console logins without MFA.
  - There is an active CloudWatch alarm associated with the filter.
  - The SNS topic has at least one valid subscription.

If any of these criteria are not met, the audit will fail.
